{{Quote|True to a longstanding tradition, Nugtosh has taken his position atop the starting spire!|Fodesinbeed Annodue|<ref name="Star Wars (1998) 15" />}}
'''Nugtosh''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nugtosh's species|alien]] individual who was known to preside over [[Podracing/Legends|podraces]] on [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], including the [[Vinta Harvest Classic/Legends|Vinta Harvest Classic]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].

==Biography==
Nugtosh, a mysterious skeletal [[Nugtosh's species|alien]], lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] as a vagrant.<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> His origin and age were unknown to the Malistarians. Sometime prior to the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], Nugtosh made a promise to not sabotage the [[Podracing/Legends|podrace]] track during the annual [[Vinta Harvest Classic/Legends|Vinta Harvest Classic]], in exchange for the opportunity to make an appearance at the beginning of each race. Nugtosh's appearance during each race became a respected permanent fixture of podraces on Malastare.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> Nugtosh was considered good luck by the podracers and fans.<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nugtosh made his first appearance in the [[1999]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]''.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode I Racer">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref> His name was identified in ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', which also offered a backstory to the character, which among other things stated that Nugtosh's gender was unknown.<ref name="Prima" /> However, Nugtosh's entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]], explicitly states Nugtosh is a male.<ref name="TCSWE" />

Nugtosh appears to be an anagram of the word ''gunshot'', probably a reference to the parallel of Nugtosh's climbing of the spire to the real-world use of a gunshot to usher in a race.
